journal_entry.reason: Capitalize purchase price and costs to bring inventory to present location and condition (inbound freight and shipping insurance) into Inventories — merchandise. Expense post-acquisition storage and outbound delivery costs as period expenses. Record accounts payable for the on-account purchase and reduce cash for all cash payments.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Target purchased merchandise on account for $1,000,000 and incurred additional costs. Under IAS 2.11, the cost of inventories comprises all costs of purchase and other costs incurred in bringing the inventories to their present location and condition. The freight to warehouse ($120,000) and shipping insurance ($30,000) are necessary to bring the merchandise to the warehouse and are capitalized into inventory cost, totaling $1,150,000. Per IAS 2.16(b), abnormal amounts of wasted materials, labor, or other production costs, and storage costs (unless necessary in the production process) are excluded from inventory cost and recognized as expenses in the period incurred. Post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) is a period cost, not a production-related storage cost. Outbound delivery freight ($50,000) is a selling cost incurred after the inventory is ready for sale and is expensed per IAS 2.16(b). Total cash paid is $220,000.
